customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety — Richard is cooperative, self-directed, and transactional. He knows what he wants (add-on panels for a new addition) and is not skeptical of the process. His only stated uncertainty is locating his electric bill.
unresolved_concerns: Electric bill not collected — appointment explicitly conditioned on bill receipt. Richard's closing line ('If I can't get it, I'll call you back') signals he may not send it before Wednesday.
Orphaned_flow 'Is your system working?' check was never completed. Closer does not know whether Richard's existing solar system is functional. If non-functional, this is a hard DQ at proposal.
Title/co-owner status unknown — Parker never asked who else is on the title or involved in the decision. A co-owner not present on Wednesday is an appointment risk.

Addition dimensions (stated as '4 by 24 by 24' by Parker at 04:32) are unverified — Richard never stated these figures in the call. Source is unknown. Closer should confirm dimensions with Richard at the start of the appointment before building on them.
sensitive_topics_raised: Existing solar system — company name unknown (Richard says it 'went back up last year' and doesn't recall the company name). Closer should probe gently.

Cleared lot with former tree coverage — satellite image will show old tree coverage, not current cleared state. Closer must not rely on satellite image for the addition lot without flagging this to the design team.
Electric heat and air conditioning disclosed — high-load profile relevant to add-on sizing. Closer should factor this into the design conversation.
exact_promises_rep_made: Closer will email the design so Richard can see it (04:08) — ambiguous whether this means during the call or before. Closer should clarify and not assume Richard expects a pre-call design delivery.
Parker noted the cleared lot and addition location in the system notes (04:40) — closer should verify these notes are accurate and complete.
closer_should_know: The satellite image for Richard's property will show the old tree-covered lot on the left side — that lot is now cleared and the addition is built there. The image is wrong for the add-on design. Confirm current lot state and addition dimensions (Parker stated '4 by 24 by 24' but Richard never confirmed these figures) before building the design. Also: the orphaned_flow system-working check was skipped — ask Richard early whether his existing system is currently producing normally, before investing time in the add-on design.
